description: §8.3 real GROUP BY aggregate (RUNTIME, sqlEngine:"real"). regionTotals() runs `SELECT region, SUM(amount) AS total FROM sales GROUP BY region ORDER BY region`.all(); the real Bun.SQL engine groups the rows by region and sums amount per group, yielding [{region:east,total:20},{region:west,total:15}]. The stub returns the three raw seed rows untouched (no grouping, no SUM) — so the aggregated result passes ONLY on the real engine.
rationale: §8.3 `.all()` = Row[]. GROUP BY + SUM is a set-oriented computation the stub structurally cannot express (it returns the seed rows verbatim). Fork B loose-infer: sales(id,region,amount) from the seed rows. The aggregate collapses 3 rows into 2 grouped rows with computed totals — empirically the stub yields all 3 raw rows.
